title: Minari
logline: A Korean American family pursues the American Dream by starting a farm in rural Arkansas in the 1980s, while a young boy with a heart condition navigates his relationship with his traditional grandmother and watches his parents' marriage strain under the weight of their aspirations.
antagonistOrConflict: The harsh realities of pursuing the American Dream - financial strain, cultural displacement, family discord, and the constant threat of failure
themes: The cost of ambition on family

actBreaks.description: Grandmother Soonja arrives, fundamentally changing the family dynamic and establishing the central relationships that will drive the story forward.

validation.ruling: The scene shows Soonja integrating into the family dynamic through card games and shared meals, marking the end of the initial adjustment period and establishing the new family structure that will drive the second act conflicts.

actBreaks.description: The family dinner with Paul marks a moment of temporary stability before the final crisis, as David and Soonja have reconciled and the farm seems to be finding its footing.

validation.ruling: Paul's dinner visit reveals his prison tattoos and past while Soonja shows signs of her declining condition, representing a major shift as external help arrives just as the family's internal struggles intensify, setting up the final act's crisis.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality:
This is a beautifully patient, character-driven narrative that unfolds like memory itself - episodic yet cohesive, building emotional weight through accumulated small moments rather than dramatic plot turns. The structure mirrors the rhythms of rural life and childhood perception, where significant events (Soonja's arrival, David's medical visits, the barn fire) punctuate long stretches of daily routine. The script trusts in the power of specificity and cultural detail to create universal emotional resonance.

The three-act structure serves the story's emotional arc rather than plot mechanics: Act 1 establishes the family's precarious new life and introduces the central tensions, Act 2 deepens relationships (particularly David and Soonja's) while external pressures mount, and Act 3 brings crisis and resolution through loss, acceptance, and the promise of renewal symbolized by the minari.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is deliberately contemplative, with an average scene length of just over one page creating a mosaic-like structure. Key sequences like the tornado (scenes 12-15), Soonja's stroke (scenes 96-109), and the barn fire (scenes 132-137) are given breathing room to develop emotional impact. The script uses seasonal progression and the farm's cycles to create natural rhythm, with moments of crisis punctuating longer stretches of character development and cultural observation.

incitingIncident.description: The family arrives at a single-wide trailer home in a vast meadow, with Monica questioning what this place is and Jacob declaring it their new home.

validation.ruling: Scene #1 is just the family traveling and doesn't shatter the status quo. Scene #2 shows the family arriving at their new trailer home in a vast meadow, with Monica questioning what this place is and Jacob declaring it their new home - this is the moment their ordinary world is shattered as they confront their new reality.

midpoint.description: David plays on rocks over the water while Soonja weeds the growing minari sprouts in the stream, explaining how minari is wonderful food that grows like weeds and can heal anyone, rich or poor, as David sings and dances about minari.

validation.ruling: At the creek, Soonja teaches David about minari while he experiences a heart episode, and she gives him wisdom about not hiding from things but seeing them - this represents David's shift from rejecting his grandmother to beginning to understand and accept her, marking his transformation from reactive to proactive in their relationship.

climax.description: Smoke and flames separate Jacob and Monica in the burning barn until Jacob finds her crawling on the ground and they escape together, while Soonja turns toward the road.

validation.ruling: Scene #135 shows Jacob starting to fight the fire but isn't the peak confrontation. Scene #137 depicts smoke and flames separating Jacob and Monica in the burning barn until Jacob finds her crawling on the ground and they escape together - this is the highest dramatic tension where the family faces potential death and Jacob must choose between his crops and his family.

scenes.heading: INT. STATION WAGON - DAY (TRAVELING)(YEAR 1983 - SPRING)
scenes.summary: The Yi family drives through Arkansas following a U-Haul truck, with young David asking his mother Monica where his father is taking them.
scenes.turningPointNote: Opening scene establishing the family's arrival and uncertain journey to their new life

scenes.heading: EXT. THE FARM - MEADOW - DAY
scenes.summary: The family arrives at a single-wide trailer home in a vast meadow, with Monica questioning what this place is and Jacob declaring it their new home.

scenes.heading: INT. MOBILE HOME - DAY
scenes.summary: David and Anne explore the empty mobile home while Monica voices disappointment that Jacob didn't find a house in a big town as promised.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE FARM - MEADOW - DAY (LATER)
scenes.summary: Jacob shows Monica the quality of the dirt on their land, explaining he chose this place for farming while Monica questions the distance to amenities.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOBILE HOME - DAY
scenes.summary: Monica struggles to climb into the mobile home and refuses Jacob's help, telling him this isn't what they agreed upon.

scenes.heading: INT. MOBILE HOME - DAY
scenes.summary: Monica looks around the mobile home and tells David that every place they move is worse and worse.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Monica checks David's heart condition while Anne asks about hanging up grandmother's picture, but Monica says they're not staying and discusses needing a hospital nearby.

scenes.heading: EXT. HATCHERY - DAY
scenes.summary: The family arrives at a chicken hatchery where workers guide a poultry truck into the loading dock.

scenes.heading: INT. HATCHERY SEXING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Mr. Hayashi introduces Jacob and Monica to the Asian workers at the hatchery, and Monica discovers there are other Koreans working there.

scenes.heading: EXT. HATCHERY - DAY
scenes.summary: Jacob explains to David that male chicks are discarded in an incinerating tower because they're useless, as a dead chick falls from the tower.

scenes.heading: INT. HATCHERY SEXING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Jacob teaches David and Anne how to sex chickens while Monica criticizes his work pace, leading to a tense exchange between the parents.

scenes.heading: INT. STATION WAGON - DAY
scenes.summary: The family drives home under green skies as Monica suggests moving to Rogers where other Koreans live, but Jacob insists on their farming deal.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOBILE HOME - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jacob secures loose objects under the trailer as furious winds from the green tornado skies threaten their mobile home.

scenes.heading: INT. MOBILE HOME - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The family prepares for a tornado as the power goes out, with Jacob explaining they may need to flee because mobile homes can fly away in tornadoes.

scenes.heading: INT. STATION WAGON - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The family sits in their car listening to tornado warnings on the radio while Jacob studies a map to determine if they're in the tornado's path.

scenes.heading: INT. DAVID’S ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: David wakes up after the tornado threat has passed, having wet the bed, and discovers paper airplanes scattered on the floor from the night before.

scenes.heading: INT. MOBILE HOME - DAY
scenes.summary: Anne and Monica prepare the mobile home by cleaning and arranging Korean decorative items, including a wedding picture of Jacob and Monica.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE FARM - MEADOW - DAY
scenes.summary: Dowsing Dan demonstrates water divining with a dowsing stick for Jacob and David, offering to find water wells for payment.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE FARM - WELL SPOT - DAY
scenes.summary: Jacob rejects the dowsing method and teaches David to find water using logic by identifying low places where water naturally flows.

scenes.heading: INT. MOBILE HOME - DAY
scenes.summary: Monica rearranges David's room to make space for her mother's bed and prepares a dresser drawer.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE FARM - WELL SPOT - DAY
scenes.summary: Jacob continues digging the well and tells David that digging a well is like life - you must stick to one spot until you reach water.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOBILE HOME - NEAR BARN - DAY
scenes.summary: Monica burns trash in a fire barrel with Anne, discussing buying new shoes for grandmother's arrival when a glass bottle explodes in the fire.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE FARM - WELL SPOT - DAY
scenes.summary: Jacob successfully finds water in the well he dug and celebrates with David, proving they used their minds instead of dowsing.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWN STREETS - DAY
scenes.summary: The family visits Holt, a tiny one-street town with a few small shops and a bank.

scenes.heading: INT. CRESCENT’S DEPARTMENT STORE - DAY
scenes.summary: Anne tries on new shoes at a small rural clothing store while a kind sales lady compliments her appearance.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLT BANK - DAY
scenes.summary: Jacob signs a bank loan with Randy Boomer, who encourages him about farming and Reagan's policies supporting farmers.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jacob commits financially to the farm by taking out a loan, escalating the stakes

scenes.heading: INT. MOBILE HOME - DAY
scenes.summary: Monica chops a thick rope with a butcher knife on a cutting board in the kitchen.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOBILE HOME - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul delivers a John Deere tractor to Jacob, who pays him around two thousand dollars in cash while David watches.

scenes.heading: INT. MOBILE HOME - DAY
scenes.summary: Monica threads rope through a wooden piece while watching Paul give Jacob an unwelcome bear hug outside near the new tractor.

scenes.heading: INT. MASTER BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Jacob explains to Monica that the tractor is an investment for their farm, promising they won't work at the hatchery in three years.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE FARM - LATE DAY
scenes.summary: Jacob drives his tractor proudly across the land while Monica and the children follow on foot, then Monica builds a tree swing.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOBILE HOME - DUSK
scenes.summary: Jacob washes off dirt from farming while explaining to Anne that Korean immigrants will create demand for Korean vegetables.

scenes.heading: INT. MASTER BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Monica cleans David's ears while a Korean church service plays on cassette, asking him why he doesn't like his grandmother.

scenes.heading: INT. MOBILE HOME - DAY
scenes.summary: Grandmother Soonja arrives at the mobile home with suitcases, greeting Anne warmly while David hides behind Monica.
scenes.turningPointNote: Soonja's arrival introduces a new family dynamic and cultural tension

scenes.heading: INT. MOBILE HOME - DAY
scenes.summary: Soonja unpacks Korean food items including red pepper flakes and dried anchovies, making Monica emotional, and forces money on Monica despite her protests.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE FARM’S HORIZON - DAY
scenes.summary: Jacob and Paul work in the field planting cucumber sprouts while Paul suggests an exorcism for the land.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WELL SPOT - DAY
scenes.summary: The well Jacob dug continues to hum with water.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Monica forces David to drink bitter Korean herbal medicine that Soonja brought, despite his protests about the taste.

scenes.heading: INT. DAVID’S ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Soonja privately removes minari seeds from a hidden packet in her clothes from the airplane.

scenes.heading: INT. ANNE’S ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: David refuses to sleep in his room because of Soonja's smell, leading to a conflict where Jacob threatens punishment for disrespecting grandmother.

scenes.heading: INT. DAVID’S ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: David lies awake plugging his nose and ears to block out Soonja's snoring in their shared room.

scenes.heading: INT. MOBILE HOME - DAY
scenes.summary: Soonja tries to connect with David over food and Mountain Dew while he remains resistant, and she teaches the children a Korean card game.

scenes.heading: INT. HATCHERY - DAY
scenes.summary: At the hatchery, Monica works slowly and carefully while discussing with Mrs. Oh why there's no Korean church in the area.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE MEADOW - DAY
scenes.summary: David and Anne walk with Soonja through the meadow, whispering that she can't read and isn't like a real grandmother.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - DAY
scenes.summary: Anne warns David to be careful as they follow Soonja toward the woods despite her concerns.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE MINARI CREEK - DAY
scenes.summary: Soonja leads the children to a creek where she wants to plant minari seeds, while Anne warns about snakes and David climbs down despite the danger.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOBILE HOME - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Monica discovers a lumber delivery and learns from Jacob that it's for building a barn, not the storm shelter she expected.

scenes.heading: INT. MASTER BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Anne presents Monica with a written list of Soonja's bad behavior while David eavesdrops and defends himself.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The family eats Korean food while watching Korean music videos, with Soonja discussing planting minari and enjoying Mountain Dew.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE TOWN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A montage of the quiet Arkansas town at night as the Korean love song fades and Monica calls David to pray.

scenes.heading: INT. DAVID’S ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Monica tells David a bedtime story about children praying to see heaven in their dreams and being healed, asking if he wants to try, while Soonja comments disapprovingly from her nearby bed.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Monica practices chicken sexing while Jacob draws barn plans, with Jacob criticizing her technique and acknowledging her loneliness without friends.

scenes.heading: INT. MASTER BEDROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Jacob puts on a necktie in the master bedroom.

scenes.heading: INT. DAVID’S ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Monica and Soonja change David's bed after he wet it, with family discussion about his bedwetting and Soonja mispronouncing 'penis' while David corrects her.

scenes.heading: INT. FIRST BAPTIST CHURCH - DAY
scenes.summary: The Yi family attends church service where Monica tithes a hundred dollars despite Jacob's disapproving look, while a boy named Johnnie stares at David.

scenes.heading: INT. FIRST BAPTIST CHURCH/FELLOWSHIP HALL - DAY
scenes.summary: At the church fellowship hall, Johnnie asks David why his face is flat while Monica struggles to connect with parishioners due to her limited English, and Jacob stands isolated.

scenes.heading: INT. STATION WAGON - DAY (TRAVELING)
scenes.summary: Driving home from church, the family discusses Monica's English struggles and large tithe, while David notices Soonja holding the stolen hundred dollar bill, and they encounter Paul dragging his wheeled cross.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE MOBILE HOME - NEAR BARN - DAY
scenes.summary: Anne tends a fire in the trash barrel outside the mobile home, with smoke enveloping her momentarily.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE TRAILER - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul arrives to help build the barn but dismisses Jacob's plans as unnecessary, then gets distracted by a whippoorwill's call before agreeing to start construction.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: David confronts Soonja about not being like a real grandmother because she doesn't make cookies, say nice things, or wear proper clothes, while she watches wrestling in polka dot boxers.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE FARM - DAY
scenes.summary: Jacob and Paul work to deepen the well using a thirty-foot auger Jacob built, with Paul questioning Jacob's methods for finding groundwater.

scenes.heading: INT. THE HATCHERY - DAY
scenes.summary: Monica works slowly at the hatchery, doubting her chicken sexing abilities while Jacob's station remains empty.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE FARM - DAY
scenes.summary: Jacob removes the auger from the well and finds only white clay with no water, leading Paul to suggest calling dowsers while Jacob asks when it will rain.

scenes.heading: INT. DAVID’S ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: David empties his herbal medicine cup and fills it with urine to trick Soonja into drinking it instead of mountain water, then gives it to her while she watches wrestling.

scenes.heading: EXT. FARM - DAY
scenes.summary: David runs excitedly but then slows down and holds his heart as palpitations begin, catching his breath while smiling.

scenes.heading: INT. MOBILE HOME - NIGHT
scenes.summary: David kneels with arms raised as punishment while Jacob and Monica demand he properly apologize to Soonja, but David refuses saying she's not a real grandma, leading to extended punishment.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOBILE HOME - NIGHT
scenes.summary: David walks outside in moonlight to find a replacement stick for his punishment, comparing two sticks before selecting a thinner one.

scenes.heading: INT. MOBILE HOME - NIGHT
scenes.summary: While waiting for David, Jacob, Monica, and Soonja discuss his behavior, with Monica blaming their hillbilly environment and Jacob defending their location as Soonja calls it heaven.

scenes.heading: INT. MASTER BATHROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jacob cannot raise his arms to remove his shirt due to exhaustion from farm work, so Monica helps him undress and later washes his hair while he sits in the tub.

scenes.heading: INT. MOBILE HOME/CAR PORT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jacob and Monica practice chicken sexing together outside, with Jacob teaching her to trust her first instinct without doubt and have absolute faith in herself when working.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOBILE HOME - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jacob sends Monica inside while he heads somewhere on the land alone at night.

scenes.heading: EXT. FARM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jacob secretly turns on water flow from the main water line to irrigate his crops using flexible pipes in the shadows.

scenes.heading: EXT. HATCHERY - MORNING
scenes.summary: Monica walks to work at the hatchery alone in the morning.

scenes.heading: EXT. MINARI FIELD - MORNING
scenes.summary: Soonja tends to the growing minari sprouts in the field.

scenes.heading: INT. THE BARN - MORNING (WEEKS LATER)
scenes.summary: Jacob and Paul organize crates in the newly built barn when Monica calls Jacob to take a phone call from Dallas, causing Jacob to run to the house.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE FARM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Jacob returns from the phone call visibly upset and tells Paul to take the crates back, revealing the Dallas buyer has cancelled their order.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Dallas produce deal falls through, threatening Jacob's entire farming venture and forcing him back to the hatchery

scenes.heading: INT. THE BARN - DAY (MOMENTS LATER)
scenes.summary: Jacob and Paul move crates back into the barn with Jacob working angrily, learning the buyer switched to California suppliers and expressing distrust of Korean people in big cities.

scenes.heading: EXT. HATCHERY - DAY
scenes.summary: The hatchery's tower of death grinds at full power with black smoke and flesh flies hovering for food.

scenes.heading: INT. HATCHERY - DAY
scenes.summary: Monica works very fast at chicken sexing while Jacob appears clumsy and tired, with Mrs. Oh noticing Monica's improved speed and Jacob's decline.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE FARM - DAY
scenes.summary: The irrigation pipe system slows to a drip and then stops making sound as the water runs out.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Anne tries the tap water in the bathroom but nothing comes out.

scenes.heading: INT. MOBILE HOME - LIVING ROOM
scenes.summary: Anne tells David to change for the church picnic while he wears oversized polka dot boxers, but David reports there's no water, and Soonja questions why it matters what a little boy wears.

scenes.heading: INT. HATCHERY - DAY
scenes.summary: Jacob drops a crate of chickens at the hatchery due to sore arms, with Monica helping him pick them up while Mrs. Oh warns about throwing away injured chicks and Mr. H watches disapprovingly.

scenes.heading: INT. DAVID’S ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: David strains to lift a fallen dresser drawer back into place and discovers blood pooling around his foot, calling for help as Soonja arrives to stop the bleeding with socks.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Soonja dresses David's wound with cotton balls and gauze while Anne watches for the church bus, with Soonja insisting Anne go to the picnic and leave David behind.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE FARM - DAY
scenes.summary: David shows Soonja a fast walking technique while she suggests they run together to a distant tree, but David holds his heart and says he can't run.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - DAY
scenes.summary: Soonja and David walk into the woods together, following a path toward the creek.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE MINARI CREEK - DAY
scenes.summary: David plays on rocks over the water while Soonja weeds the growing minari sprouts in the stream, explaining how minari is wonderful food that grows like weeds and can heal anyone, rich or poor, as David sings and dances about minari.
scenes.turningPointNote: David and Soonja bond over the minari, representing the connection between generations and the healing power of their Korean heritage

scenes.heading: INT. MOBILE HOME - LATE DAY
scenes.summary: Jacob returns home angry, making a loud bang, and tries the tap by habit but finds no water, discovering buckets of water while Soonja greets him.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOBILE HOME - LATE DAY
scenes.summary: Jacob changes from sandals to boots to check the irrigation pipes while David plays with a toy car nearby, trying to show his father the road he made in the dirt.

scenes.heading: INT. MOBILE HOME - KITCHEN - LATE DAY
scenes.summary: Monica and Soonja discuss the water situation in the kitchen surrounded by buckets of water and minari, with Monica revealing Jacob used their household water for crops and will likely borrow more money from the bank.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOBILE HOME - LATE DAY
scenes.summary: David spies through the window on Monica and Soonja's conversation without them knowing.

scenes.heading: INT. MOBILE HOME - KITCHEN - LATE DAY
scenes.summary: Soonja tells Monica that David helped carry water and is stronger than she thinks, but Monica reveals David needs heart surgery at any moment and apologizes for leaving Soonja alone to watch him.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE FARM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jacob searches his crops at night with a spotlight and finds an overripe eggplant crawling with bugs, which he tosses down in frustration.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE MOBILE HOME - NIGHT
scenes.summary: During a thunderstorm, David lies in bed rubbing his heart when Soonja asks why he can't sleep, and he explains Monica said he could see heaven if he prays in his dreams.

scenes.heading: INT. DAVID’S ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: David wakes thinking he wet the bed but discovers Soonja has wet the bed instead, then leads Anne to see Soonja who wakes trembling with her face not right.
scenes.turningPointNote: Soonja shows clear signs of a medical emergency, likely a stroke, fundamentally changing the family's situation

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: David asks Anne why Soonja is acting strange, and Anne decides they should call their mother from church while getting ready for the bus.

scenes.heading: INT. DAVID’S ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Anne quietly gets David's clothes from the dresser while Soonja appears to sleep, but after they leave, Soonja opens her eyes and stares at the dresser as if menaced by something she sees there.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Anne hurriedly washes her face in a bucket and runs out of the bathroom.

scenes.heading: INT. SANCTUARY - DAY
scenes.summary: At church, Brother Roy concludes his sermon about Jesus's return while Anne whispers to David that she's going to call their mother and leaves him alone in the sanctuary.

scenes.heading: EXT./INT. CHURCH BUS - DAY (TRAVELING)
scenes.summary: Randy drives the church bus with kids playing while Anne tells David their parents took grandma to the hospital, and the kids mock Paul carrying his cross on the road.

scenes.heading: INT. MOBILE HOME/LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Anne talks on the phone about staying with friends while David asks about grandma, but Anne says their mother wouldn't reveal how she's doing.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWN STREETS - DAY
scenes.summary: Johnnie and David take turns riding bicycles down the street, with Johnnie encouraging David to flip off the townspeople.

scenes.heading: INT. MOTHER’S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Johnnie gives David tobacco to try and they play cards with money while spitting into a Coke can.

scenes.heading: INT. JOHNNIE’S BATHROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: David and Johnnie brush their teeth by moving their heads instead of their arms and laugh together.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Soonja sleeps in a hospital bed surrounded by flowers and monitoring equipment while Monica sits with her.

scenes.heading: INT. JOHNNIE’S HOME - MORNING
scenes.summary: Billy tells David about the previous farm owner Bucky Reed who went broke and committed suicide, while Johnnie plays on Billy's lap.

scenes.heading: EXT./INT. JOHNNIE’S HOME/STATION WAGON - DAY
scenes.summary: Jacob picks up David from Johnnie's house and they drive away while David and Anne discuss their friends' houses.

scenes.heading: INT. DAVID’S ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Anne finds Soonja staring blankly at an empty dresser, and Monica explains to Anne that Soonja had a stroke and blames herself for bringing her to America.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE FARM - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Jacob smokes a cigarette at sunset and pleads 'Please. Please.'

scenes.heading: INT. MASTER BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Dirty brown water comes out of the bathroom faucet and toilet tank makes refilling sounds.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Anne tells David while brushing teeth that their parents might divorce after checking David's heart, and suggests they each stay with one parent.

scenes.heading: INT. DAVID’S ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: David blames Soonja in Korean for everything being her fault, and she opens her eyes after he thinks she's asleep.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A snake slithers down the narrow hallway.

scenes.heading: INT. MOBILE HOME - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul joins the family for dinner and David refuses to help Soonja when she spills water, while Paul sweats from eating kimchi.

scenes.heading: INT. DAVID’S ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul performs an exorcism in David's room, dabbing oil and praying in tongues to cast out whatever Soonja sees on the dresser.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul offers to pray for Jacob and Monica's relationship, but Jacob declines the suggestion.

scenes.heading: INT. THE BARN - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul and Jacob pack produce samples and brochures for Big Country Farms in the barn.

scenes.heading: INT. MOBILE HOME - DAY
scenes.summary: Monica prepares food and gives Soonja instructions while holding an envelope of money from Korea, as Soonja accidentally drops a kitchen pot.

scenes.heading: INT. STATION WAGON - MONTAGE (TRAVELING)
scenes.summary: The family drives in silence through lakes and forests with the kids sleeping and eating snacks in the backseat.

scenes.heading: INT. STATION WAGON - DAY (TRAVELING)
scenes.summary: Anne wakes David to see the Oklahoma City skyline and Monica asks if they remember living in a bigger city.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARKING STRUCTURE ROOF - DAY (OKLAHOMA CITY)
scenes.summary: Monica criticizes Jacob for bringing the dirty produce box into the hospital, and Jacob insists he'll wait for a parking spot while she goes in first.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: David receives a sonogram showing blood spraying between his heart's ventricle wall while Jacob studies the screen and enters carrying the fruit box.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOBILE HOME - DAY
scenes.summary: Soonja walks with her cane on the path between trees toward the barn.

scenes.heading: INT. WAITING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Jacob tells Monica they came to America to save each other but instead fought so much that David was born with his condition, while Monica asks him to come with them to California.

scenes.heading: INT. DOCTOR’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: The doctor tells the family that David's heart hole is getting smaller and surgery can be ruled out, delivering unexpectedly good news.
scenes.turningPointNote: Major relief as David's heart condition improves, removing the immediate medical crisis that has been driving family decisions

scenes.heading: EXT. BARN - DAY
scenes.summary: Soonja cleans up rotting produce that's been discarded from the harvest near the barn.

scenes.heading: INT. PARKING GARAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Jacob drains ice water from bags while Monica plans to find naeng myun for the kids, and they acknowledge hearing good news for once.

scenes.heading: INT. KOREAN GROCER - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Anne and David look at pigs feet while Jacob talks and laughs with the Korean grocery store owner who examines the produce.

scenes.heading: INT. KOREAN GROCER - BACK ROOM
scenes.summary: The grocery owner reviews Jacob's farm brochure featuring David and Anne on a tractor while Jacob pitches his business idea about supplying fresh local produce.

scenes.heading: EXT. KOREAN GROCER BACK ALLEYWAY - LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Monica confronts Jacob in the alley, accusing him of choosing the farm over family even at the hospital, and threatens that if David had needed surgery, she would have left him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Monica's ultimatum crystallizes the fundamental conflict between Jacob's farm dreams and family unity, threatening the marriage

scenes.heading: EXT. MOBILE HOME - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Soonja tends a fire in the barrel but misses when throwing trash, causing it to fall out and start a small grass fire.

scenes.heading: INT. STATION WAGON - NIGHT (TRAVELING)
scenes.summary: David quietly tells Monica he prayed to see heaven and saw it, but she responds distractedly while they ride in silence.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOBILE HOME - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Fire spreads from the barrel to grass and straw bales near the barn as Soonja fights the flames with a blackened face, and Jacob arrives to see the barn catching fire.
scenes.turningPointNote: The barn fire begins, threatening to destroy Jacob's entire farming operation and livelihood

scenes.heading: INT. BARN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jacob fights the growing barn fire with a canvas tarp while Monica enters and joins the desperate effort to save the burning structure.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Soonja stands watching the barn flames grow while looking back at the car with the children inside.

scenes.heading: INT. BARN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Smoke and flames separate Jacob and Monica in the burning barn until Jacob finds her crawling on the ground and they escape together, while Soonja turns toward the road.
scenes.turningPointNote: The barn fire reaches its climax with Jacob and Monica nearly trapped, and the farm's destruction becomes complete

scenes.heading: EXT. THE DIRT ROAD - DUSK
scenes.summary: David and Anne chase after Soonja who walks down the dirt road in shame and guilt as a fire truck passes dangerously close, and David runs for the first time.
scenes.turningPointNote: David runs for the first time despite his heart condition, showing his desperation to save Soonja who is fleeing in self-imposed exile

scenes.heading: EXT. THE BARN - BEFORE DAWN
scenes.summary: The barn stands burned with scattered, charred crops in boxes on the ground.

scenes.heading: INT. MOBILE HOME - BEFORE DAWN
scenes.summary: Framed family pictures are displayed in succession showing Monica and Soonja, a family vacation, and the children with Jacob on a tractor.

scenes.heading: INT. MOBILE HOME - BEFORE DAWN
scenes.summary: David, Anne, Monica, and Jacob sleep collapsed on the floor with soot stains on their hands and clothes while Soonja sits awake in a chair watching them.

scenes.heading: EXT. FARM - DAY
scenes.summary: A diviner with a dowsing stick finds water on the land while Jacob, Monica, and Paul follow, and Jacob marks the spot with a stone like a monument.
scenes.turningPointNote: Finding water represents hope for rebuilding and Jacob's commitment to staying on the farm despite the destruction

scenes.heading: EXT. TREE SWING - LATE DAY
scenes.summary: Soonja watches David and Anne play at the tree swing while Monica and Jacob approach, and Jacob asks David to come with him somewhere.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - LATE DAY
scenes.summary: Jacob and David gather abundant minari growing in streaming water, with Jacob telling David that grandmother picked a good spot because it grows without care.
scenes.turningPointNote: The discovery of thriving minari represents resilience, Soonja's wisdom, and the family's ability to endure and grow despite hardship

structuralWarnings.text: 2 of 3 key moments were auto-corrected by Phase 1C
